claimText: Chandrayaan-3's Chandra's Surface Thermophysical Experiment (ChaSTE) payload is the first mission to successfully penetrate the soil of a celestial body to deploy a thermal probe.
evidenceExcerpt: Chandra's Surface Thermophysical Experiment (ChaSTE) of Chandrayaan-3 became the first mission to successfully penetrate the soil of a celestial body to deploy a thermal probe.
examUse: Statement-based question on the specific achievements of Chandrayaan-3 payloads.
distractorHooks: Attributing the thermal probe deployment to a different payload like ILSA or RAMBHA
Claiming it was the first to penetrate Martian soil instead of a generic celestial body/lunar soil

claimText: Chandrayaan-3 is the world's first mission to soft-land near the lunar South Pole, and its landing site is named 'Shiv Shakti' point.
evidenceExcerpt: Became the world's first mission to soft-land near the lunar South Pole. The landing site was named as 'Shiv Shakti' point.
examUse: Fact-based correct-pair or statement question regarding lunar landing sites.
distractorHooks: Naming the landing site 'Tiranga point' or 'Jawahar point'
Claiming Chandrayaan-3 landed near the lunar North Pole

claimText: Japan became the fifth country to soft-land on the Moon with its SLIM mission.
evidenceExcerpt: Japan became the fifth country to soft-land on the Moon with its SLIM mission.
examUse: Correct-pair question matching countries to their respective lunar missions or chronological order of lunar landings.
distractorHooks: Claiming Japan was the fourth country to soft-land on the Moon
Swapping the SLIM mission with Russia's Luna-25 or another country's mission

claimText: NASA's GRAIL Mission created the most detailed map of the Moon's gravity.
evidenceExcerpt: NASA's GRAIL Mission created the most detailed map of the Moon's gravity.
examUse: Correct-pair question matching space missions to their primary objectives.
distractorHooks: Attributing the GRAIL mission to ISRO or ESA
Claiming the GRAIL mission mapped lunar water ice instead of gravity

claimText: The Moon's nearside, which faces Earth, is warmer and more geologically active than the farside.
evidenceExcerpt: The Moon's nearside (facing Earth) is warmer and more geologically active than the farside.
examUse: Comparison statement question on lunar geography and geological activity.
distractorHooks: Reversing the facts by claiming the farside is warmer and more geologically active than the nearside

claimText: The far side of the Moon is tidally locked with Earth and features a thicker crust, more craters, and fewer lava plains compared to the near side.
evidenceExcerpt: Refers to the hemisphere of the Moon that always faces away from Earth because the Moon is tidally locked with Earth. It has a thicker crust, more craters, and fewer lava plains than near side.
examUse: Statement-based question on the physical characteristics of the Moon's far side.
distractorHooks: Claiming the far side has a thinner crust than the near side
Stating the far side has more lava plains (maria) than the near side
Asserting the far side is not tidally locked with Earth
